fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Wisconsin Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Wisconsin - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity wisconsin, how to transfer physical therapist license to wisconsin, physical therapist compact wisconsin, wisconsin physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Wisconsin DSPS — Physical Therapist
board.parent_dept: Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services (DSPS)
board.mailing_address: Department of Safety and Professional Services, P.O. Box 8368, Madison, WI 53708-8368

quick_facts.ce_requirement: 30 hours of continuing education per renewal cycle

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Wisconsin.

steps.title: Create a LicensE Account
steps.description: Register for an account on the Wisconsin DSPS LicensE portal at licenserequired.wi.gov. Provide your personal information and verify your email to gain access to the application system.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your LicensE account and select the application for 'Physical Therapist'. Fill out all required sections, including personal details, educational history, and respond to background questions.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts verifying the completion of your accredited physical therapy program to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Wisconsin DSPS.

steps.title: Request NPTE Scores
steps.description: Ensure your official National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE) scores are sent directly from the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT) to the Wisconsin DSPS.

steps.title: Provide Licensure Verification (if applicable)
steps.description: If you hold or have held a PT license in another state, arrange for verification of licensure to be sent directly from that state's licensing board to the Wisconsin DSPS.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee through the LicensE portal using a credit card or other accepted payment method to finalize your application submission.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Wisconsin physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $135. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Wisconsin have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Wisconsin participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Wisconsin without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Wisconsin?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 3 to 4 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Wisconsin while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Wisconsin
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Wisconsin, you must submit your application through the Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services (DSPS) online LicensE portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: To create an account and start an application on the LicensE portal, visit the portal URL and click 'Register for an Account'. Follow the prompts to provide personal information, set up security questions, and verify your email address. Once registered, log in to access the application dashboard and initiate a new Physical Therapist licensure application.
